summary:
The auriculotemporal nerve surrounds the middle meningocephalic artery and plays a role in sensory innervation of structures including teeth and temporal scalp. It does not directly supply blood vessels but rather provides sensation for specific areas like the skin overlying part of the parieto-occipital region as well as dental pulp via branches that accompany small arteries supplying these regions indirectly through anastamoses with other nerves' vascular networks. Sentence focusing only on clinically-relevant information would be:

The auriculotemporal nerve primarily supplies sensations to the cheek area and jaw, wrapping around the middle meningoencephalic artery en route from brainstem towards mandible without direct arterial function itself. To keep it concise while retaining essential details,
